code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    bb = ta.volatility.BollingerBands(df['close'], window=params['bb_window'], window_dev=params['bb_dev'])
    df['bb_upper'] = bb.bollinger_hband()
    df['bb_lower'] = bb.bollinger_lband()
    
    # Calculate RSI
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window']).rsi()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long conditions: price below lower BB and RSI oversold
    long_cond = (df['close'] < df['bb_lower']) & (df['rsi'] < params['rsi_lower'])
    
    # Short conditions: price above upper BB and RSI overbought
    short_cond = (df['close'] > df['bb_upper']) & (df['rsi'] > params['rsi_upper'])
    
    signals[long_cond] = 1
    signals[short_cond] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/USD exhibits mean-reverting behavior where extreme Bollinger Band deviations combined with RSI extremes signal high-probability reversals.
